description: Configure GPredict for a VHF/UHF contest rover by setting up three ground stations: 'Rover_EN82' (42.5000 N, 83.5000 W, 250m), 'Rover_EN81' (41.5000 N, 83.5000 W, 220m), and 'Rover_EN91' (41.5000 N, 81.5000 W, 200m). Establish a tracking module named 'Contest_Rover' featuring satellites AO-7 (7530), FO-29 (24278), SO-50 (27607), AO-91 (43017), and AO-92 (43137). The module must use a 'Polar only' layout and be bound to the 'Rover_EN82' ground station. Additionally, enable automatic TLE updates from the network. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm setting up a laptop for an amateur radio VHF contest rover. Can you configure GPredict for me? First, add three ground stations for my route: Rover_EN82 (42.5 N, 83.5 W, 250m), Rover_EN81 (41.5 N, 83.5 W, 220m), and Rover_EN91 (41.5 N, 81.5 W, 200m). Then, create a new tracking module called 'Contest_Rover' with AO-7 (7530), FO-29 (24278), SO-50 (27607), AO-91 (43017), and AO-92 (43137). I need that module's layout set to 'Polar only' so I can easily point my antennas, and bind that module to use the 'Rover_EN82' ground station instead of the default. Finally, please go into the general preferences and turn on automatic TLE data updates from the network so the tracking stays accurate while I'm on the road.
